Course Title: AI and Guest Data Privacy Risks
Category: Hospitality & Tourism: Hotels, Tourism, Travel Agencies, Food & Beverage
Who is this for: Front desk staff, hotel managers, reservation agents, concierge staff, restaurant managers, travel agents, tour operators, hospitality supervisors, guest services representatives
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Guest Services, IT, Legal, Compliance, Revenue Management, Food & Beverage
Description: Protecting personal, travel, and payment data.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with everyday hospitality operations, such as checking in guests or handling bookings, along with a willingness to learn about data privacy concepts, is all that is required to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key privacy risks associated with using AI to collect and process guest data, and explain how personal, travel, and payment information must be handled responsibly within hospitality and tourism settings. You will also apply practical strategies to protect guest data, recognise potential vulnerabilities in AI-driven systems, and ensure your organisation's practices align with relevant data protection regulations.
